author: 0libots
description: Import documents, media, archives, and web pages as Markdown using Microsoft MarkItDown. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.name: SourceDown

name: SourceDown

releases.author: 0libote
releases.description:
## What's Changed
* Add optional Docling and Marker converters by @0libote in https://github.com/0libote/SourceDown/pull/7
* Fix Marker package install spec by @0libote in https://github.com/0libote/SourceDown/pull/8


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/0libote/SourceDown/compare/1.1.3...1.2.1
releases.name: V1.2.1

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What’s new

- Added optional Docling and Marker conversion engines.
- MarkItDown remains the default; optional engines install only when enabled.
- Added a converter dropdown with file-type recommendations.
- Added `conversion_engine` to generated note frontmatter.
- Improved engine status, installation state, and output handling.
- Added `ROADMAP.md` and updated setup documentation.

## Upgrade

Update SourceDown normally. To use Docling or Marker, enable it under **Settings → SourceDown → Conversion engines**, then select **Install / update**.

Docling and Marker are larger installs and may download models on first use. Marker sidecar images are not yet copied into the vault.

**Full changelog:** https://github.com/0libote/SourceDown/compare/1.1.3...1.2.0
releases.name: V1.2.0

releases.name: V1.1.3

releases.author: 0libote
releases.description:
Moves the private MarkItDown virtual environment out of the Obsidian vault/plugin folder and into local app data.

Changes:
- Uses `%LOCALAPPDATA%\SourceDown\.venv` on Windows.
- Uses local app data equivalents on macOS and Linux.
- Reuses/upgrades the venv instead of clearing it on every install.
- Rebuilds the local venv automatically if the existing environment is incomplete or cannot be upgraded.
- Stores installed add-on state beside the shared venv so it works across vaults.
- Removes the `process.cwd()` app-data fallback and shows a clear setup error if no local app-data path can be resolved.
- Updates README and bumps the Obsidian manifest version to 1.1.2.
- Updates the release workflow to sync version files from the tag before building and releasing.
releases.name: V1.1.2
